Ad Text:
Your new company

Service and Creative Skills Australia (SaCSA) is an industry-owned, industry-led Jobs & Skills Council (JSC), funded by the Department of Employment & Workplace Relations (DEWR). We offer workforce planning, guidance, and advocacy for the following industries:



Arts
Personal Services
Retail
Tourism
Hospitality

Working within a tripartite approach, SaCSA is the critical link between industry stakeholders, government, and unions. We aim to provide a stronger, more strategic voice in ensuring Australia's Vocational Education and Training sector delivers stronger outcomes for employers, workers, learners, trainers, and educators. As the leader in workforce development initiatives for our industries, SaCSA collaborates with industry to identify and articulate current and future employment trends and develops solutions that best serve those needs.

Your new role

The General Manager, Insights & Planning is accountable to the Executive Director, Workforce Strategy and Communications for leading the Insights and Planning function, bringing together workforce data, applied research and analysis, and industry intelligence to shape forward-looking workforce strategies and influence the national skills system.

This role blends labour market and workforce planning expertise with market research capability, ensuring SaCSA's insights are co-designed with industry, analytically rigorous and translated into compelling publications, narratives, and evidence-based recommendations for the industries within SaCSA's remit, government and the broader public.
Key outcomes:

Brings together people leadership, applied research, data stewardship and strategic translation, operating at the intersection of industry co-design, quantitative and qualitative analysis, and forward-looking strategy development to ensure SaCSA's insights are credible, timely, relevant and actively used.
Provides strong, visible and empowering leadership to the Insights and Planning team, fostering a high-performing culture grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and analytical rigour.
Oversees the end-to-end development of research projects, publications, workforce planning strategies, and insights products, from research through to delivery and dissemination.



What you'll need to succeed

Demonstrated ability to lead and develop multidisciplinary research or analytics teams.
Demonstrated experience working with key workforce and occupational data models and classification frameworks (e.g. ANZSCO, OSCA and related industry datasets), with the ability to apply these in analysis, interpretation, submissions, and insight development.
Significant experience in applied research, labour market, workforce analysis, or related fields.
Strong understanding of qualitative and quantitative research methodologies.
Experience working within, or in close partnership with relevant government, statutory bodies, or policy-focused organisations (JSA, ABS, NCVER, etc).

What you need to do now

The Company

Our client is a long-established, purpose-led organisation operating within a regulated environment, with a strong reputation for governance, accountability and long-term sustainability. Having invested heavily in strengthening its risk and compliance capability in recent times, the organisation is committed to embedding a proactive, business-partnering approach to risk across the enterprise.

As such, an opportunity has arisen for a Risk & Compliance professional to join a collaborative and high-performing team in a pivotal role.

The Role

This is a broad and visible Senior Risk & Compliance Analyst position, sitting at the intersection of business operations and governance. Reporting to an experienced Senior Manager, you will play a key role in supporting the ongoing evolution of the organisation's risk and compliance frameworks while partnering closely with stakeholders to uplift capability and maturity.

Key responsibilities will include:

Working across the full risk lifecycle, including facilitating risk workshops, refining risk profiles, and assessing control effectiveness and mitigation strategies.
Contributing to assurance activities, thematic reviews and incident analysis to support a more proactive, forward-looking approach to risk management.
Translating risk and compliance data into clear, meaningful reporting for senior leadership and governance forums.
Ownership of dashboards and key metrics, ensuring outputs are accurate, insightful and support informed decision-making.
Support regulatory compliance activities, including maintaining obligations frameworks, contributing to reviews and uplift initiatives, and responding to regulatory changes.
Operating in a hands-on capacity with broad exposure across risk, compliance and governance activities.
Maximising the effectiveness of the organisation's GRC platform by improving data capture, analysis and reporting.
Assessing and enhancing system workflows, provide user support and identify opportunities to improve efficiency and generate better risk insights.

In addition, you will contribute to broader governance and resilience activities, supporting audit processes, business continuity and crisis management exercises, and helping ensure governance processes and documentation are robust and fit for purpose.

Your Profile

You are an experienced risk and compliance professional with a background in a complex or regulated environment, bringing a strong grasp of contemporary risk management practices. Comfortable working across both risk and compliance domains, you will be someone who understands how to balance technical rigour with commercial pragmatism.

You may come from financial services, superannuation, insurance or a similarly regulated sector, and will ideally have had exposure to evolving regulatory standards (such as CPS 230 or equivalent frameworks). Experience working with GRC systems such as Protecht or Archer would also be particularly beneficial.

Beyond your technical capability, you are a strong communicator who can build credibility with stakeholders across departments and disciplines and be seen as an approachable provider of clear, practical advice for the benefit of the organisation as a whole. You should be a relationship builder and naturally curious individual with a solutions-oriented mindset who is confident navigating ambiguity, with the ability to distil complex information into clear insights.

Taking ownership of your work, you will operate with a high level of accountability, and enjoy working in an environment where you can influence outcomes and contribute to continuous improvement. This role will suit someone looking to step into a broader, more commercially engaged risk position with strong exposure to senior stakeholders and enterprise-wide initiatives.

You will be joining a collaborative and high-performing team where risk is genuinely valued as a driver of better decision-making, rather than just a control function. For a Risk and Compliance professional looking to take their career to the next level and with a business with a great culture and reputation, this is an outstanding opportunity.

Are you a proactive data professional ready to hit the ground running? We are looking for a Data Management Analyst to join our Data Management team. This role is perfect for a self-starter who work autonomously to enhance data quality, document critical metadata, and partner with stakeholders to embed robust data management practices across the organisation with minimal supervision.




Role: Data Management Analyst

Location: Canberra, Sydney or Melbourne (hybrid)

Job Type: 6 month contract (maternity cover)

Start Date: ASAP




Responsibilities:

Data Quality Management: Define Data Quality requirements, develop automated rules, and perform root cause analysis to remediate issues and improve trust in enterprise data.

Metadata & Cataloguing: Drive the population and maintenance of the enterprise data catalogue, documenting technical, business, and lineage metadata for key assets.

Framework & Policy Development: Contribute to the creation and periodic updates of data management policies and standards, ensuring alignment with the data vision and regulatory frameworks.

Sensitive Data Handling: Apply standardised classification frameworks to identify and secure sensitive or regulated data in collaboration with security and compliance teams.

Stakeholder Engagement & Stewardship: Educate and engage Data Owners and Stewards on their accountabilities, integrating them into operational governance through KPIs and maturity assessments.




Experience:

Industry Expertise: Experience in data management, data quality, or data governance roles. While experience in the superannuation industry is highly regarded, we value those from other regulated backgrounds who understand complex data environments.

Analytical Power: Proven ability to explore and analyse data using SQL, Excel, and visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau), as well as interpreting data flow diagrams.

Technical Knowledge: Demonstrated understanding of data governance frameworks, data modeling, and privacy requirements.

Agile Proficiency: Experience delivering data solutions within Agile frameworks such as SAFe, Scrum, or Lean.

Effective Communication: Strong verbal and written skills with a "Working with People" mindset - showing the ability to share information proactively and build strong connections across the team.

Autonomous Execution: Ability to use sound judgment to organise your own work, set priorities, and deliver assigned tasks independently.

Who are Vescient? 

We are an innovative and dynamic Professional Services Consultancy. We solve problems and create tangible and sustainable value for our Clients. Our people are exceptional. We are dedicated, capable, passionate, and adaptable. We believe the key to our ongoing success is our culture of teamwork and collaboration.

When you join Vescient, you join our diverse family. Our core strength is born from our varied skills, personalities, experiences, and cultures. We empower our family members to be themselves, nurture them to develop, celebrate with them when they achieve amazing outcomes, and encourage them when they experience setbacks. We also place our absolute trust in them.

Our people are exceptional. We are dedicated, capable, passionate, and adaptable. We believe the key to our ongoing success is our culture of teamwork, collaboration and domain expertise. 

Vescient offers technology-agnostic services and has delivered significant digital transformation projects including ERP systems implementation, automation, asset management and organisational design. We provide end-to-end lifecycle management for physical, digital and intellectual assets.

About the role

Vescient' is expanding its team in Melbourne, and is looking for Digital Project Managers and Change Mangers with experience in managing technology and systems-related projects in complex and evolving organisational environments.

The ideal candidate will have a technical mindset and at least 5 years’ experience delivering digital projects or enabling them via change management. Candidates with experience in Information Security, Systems Architecture and Design, data management and migration, Business Analysis, Business Process mapping, systems integration, APIs and interface delivery, and coding will be welcome.

As a member of the Digital and Security Team, you will also support the provision of Vescient’s Managed Secure IT services (MSIT). We provide secure IT services to clients across the country, who require everything from onboarding, 1st, 2nd and 3rd line support to digital and security capability enhancements and account and client management. You will be expected to bring your unique skills and experience to enhancing the value we provide our clients in MSIT. 

As part of a business with a broad portfolio of clients and projects, you will be able to apply your knowledge and experience across industries and projects, and to leverage your technical background and experiences to provide support to other areas of our consulting business, which include Business Advisory, Program and Project Management, Transformation, and Asset Management and Engineering.

The ideal candidate will be an Australian citizen with the ability to gain and retain a Defence security clearance. 

The exact nature and complexity of projects and programs may vary depending on our client engagements but at a general level, the work will involve:

Planning, directing, and co-ordinating activities to manage and implement a contracted program of work or project scope from proposal initiation through to final operational stage including the transition into “business-as-usual”. 

Planning and driving scoping, requirements definition and prioritisation activities for large, complex initiatives.

Obtaining input from, and formal agreement to, requirements from a diverse range of stakeholders.

Working with stakeholders to identify and put in place mechanisms to measure, deliver and communicate program / project benefits.

Defining, documenting and successfully delivering complex projects (typically with significant business, political, or high-profile impact, and high-risk dependencies).

Engaging with and providing ongoing reporting to management and senior stakeholders.

Negotiating with stakeholders to manage competing priorities and conflicts.

Managing project / program timeframes, budget and scope with mechanisms to track, manage changes to requirements.

As part of any role with Vescient, you will also proactively take part in internal continuous improvement activities as well as support or lead business development activities.

Essential Capabilities, Behaviours, Skills or Experience

Demonstrated behaviours aligned to Vescient’s values of Integrity, Courage, Teamwork, Respect, Initiative, Leadership and Loyalty.

Demonstrated knowledge, experience and ability to apply technology-centred program and project development and delivery methodologies as is appropriate for the work.

Demonstrated knowledge, experience and ability in business case development, initiating, delivering and closing ICT programs or projects, inclusive of the identification and management of associated business, program and project risks and business case realisation.

Demonstrated ability and desire to effectively engage and communicate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders across multiple levels of the organisation, and across multiple organisations.

Demonstrated ability and desire to integrate and collaborate with other disciplines that are required to deliver a project or program successfully (e.g. business analysis, change management).

Demonstrated ability and desire to effectively manage stakeholders and to identify, select and assign resources (e.g. team members, contractors, vendors etc) to deliver expected outcomes.

Demonstrated time management and organisational skills.

Demonstrated negotiation and diplomacy skills.

Demonstrated continuous learning and improvement mindset.

Desirable Skills or Experience

Demonstrated skills or experience in any of the following:

Information strategy: Information security, information governance, information assurance

Business change management: Business analysis, business modelling, requirements definition and management, organisational capability development, organisation design and implementation

Cyber Security: Governance, Risk, Compliance, infrastructure compliance, cloud security, application whitelisting, firewall and critical system segregation, and information management security practices

What we offer

Working on interesting, diverse, and challenging projects across a broad range of clients and industry sectors.

Collaborating and influencing decisions regarding the growth of Vescient.

Competitive remuneration.

Senior Technical / Data Analyst | Azure & Data | Contract | Canberra

Your new company

The organisation provides leadership in information and records management across government, setting standards, issuing retention and disposal authorities, and advising agencies on best‑practice governance. It also selects, preserves, and makes available nationally significant records to support accountability, transparency, research, and public access.



Your new role

As a Senior Technical Analyst, you will support the delivery of a major enterprise data initiative focused on improving data quality, integration, and reporting capability. Working closely with project delivery teams, you will analyse complex source systems, develop detailed data maps and models, and support the implementation of an enterprise data warehouse built on Microsoft Fabric and Azure. You will play a critical role in translating business rules into clear technical requirements, supporting ETL/ELT pipeline design, producing high‑quality technical documentation, and ensuring downstream Power BI reporting requirements are met. This is a 6‑month contract based in Canberra W/ extension options.



What you'll need to succeed

To be successful, you will have:

Strong experience as a Technical Analyst or Technical Business Analyst in data‑heavy environments
Solid SQL skills for data validation, testing, and analysis
Experience working with Azure cloud platforms and Azure DevOps
Understanding of ETL/ELT pipelines and enterprise data warehousing concepts
Experience producing data models, ERDs, lineage, and source‑to‑target mappings
Exposure to Power BI and downstream reporting requirements
Strong documentation and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to obtain and maintain Baseline Security Clearance (Australian citizenship required)

What you'll get in return

You'll secure a competitive daily rate and the opportunity to contribute to a high‑profile, complex data program using modern cloud and analytics technologies. This role offers meaningful technical responsibility, exposure to enterprise‑scale data challenges, and a collaborative project environment based in Canberra.



What you need to do now

Ad Text:
We are looking for an experienced Business Intelligence Developer to join our IT/ERP team, someone who genuinely enjoys working with data, cares about how things are built, and likes turning messy inputs into insights the business actually uses.

This is a hands‑on, delivery‑focused role. You will be deep in SQL, models and pipelines, working across the full BI stack and partnering with the business to solve real problems with data. It's not a junior role, and it's not just charts and colours, you will be expected to understand how everything fits together and why it's built that way.

What your day will look like

No two days are identical, but you'll spend your time doing things like:

Writing solid, performant T‑SQL to support reporting and ad‑hoc data requests
Building and maintaining Tabular models and ETL pipelines, and keeping them tidy and efficient
Tuning and optimising across the BI stack - queries, models and refresh performance
Working through data challenges with stakeholders and translating requirements into practical solutions
Researching and experimenting with AI in an enterprise context, focusing on what's genuinely useful (not just shiny)
Developing and supporting AI platforms and tools that integrate cleanly with existing BI solutions


What we're looking for

This role suits someone who already has solid experience in a BI or data role and enjoys being hands‑on. You'll bring:

Strong, practical T‑SQL skills
Advanced experience with Tabular models and Power BI
Hands‑on experience with SSIS and Git‑based workflows
Experience working with the .NET stack for data or platform development
Advanced proficiency in Excel (because sometimes it's still the right tool)
Why this role

You will be joining a team that values clean data, well‑built solutions and thoughtful design. There's room to experiment with BI and AI in a sensible way, influence how solutions are built, and still spend most of your time doing meaningful, hands‑on technical work not just talking about it.

It's not a junior role, and it's not just charts and colours, you will be expected to understand how everything fits together and why it's built that way.

What your day will look like

No two days are identical, but you'll spend your time doing things like:

Writing solid, performant T‑SQL to support reporting and ad‑hoc data requests
Building and maintaining Tabular models and ETL pipelines, and keeping them tidy and efficient
Tuning and optimising across the BI stack - queries, models and refresh performance
Working through data challenges with stakeholders and translating requirements into practical solutions
Researching and experimenting with AI in an enterprise context, focusing on what's genuinely useful (not just shiny)
Developing and supporting AI platforms and tools that integrate cleanly with existing BI solutions


What we're looking for

This role suits someone who already has solid experience in a BI or data role and enjoys being hands‑on. You'll bring:

Strong, practical T‑SQL skills
Advanced experience with Tabular models and Power BI
Hands‑on experience with SSIS and Git‑based workflows
Experience working with the .NET stack for data or platform development
Advanced proficiency in Excel (because sometimes it's still the right tool)
Why this role

You will be joining a team that values clean data, well‑built solutions and thoughtful design. There's room to experiment with BI and AI in a sensible way, influence how solutions are built, and still spend most of your time doing meaningful, hands‑on technical work not just talking about it.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer / Senior Analytics Engineer
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer

At Elula, we partner with some of Australia's top financial institutions to tackle a wide range of business challenges through cutting-edge AI-as-a-service (AIaaS). Our vision? To make the complex simple and easy, delivering amazing customer experiences and personalisation that matters.

Our products leverage machine learning, productionised models, data pipelines, explainable AI, and rich visualisations to drive customer engagement, retention and insights. We're constantly pushing the envelope, uncovering the ever-evolving needs of customers and staying ahead of the curve.

Join a team of some of the brightest minds in data science and engineering; experts with years of experience and a passion for delivering smarter decision-making. It's a fast-paced, high-performing and collaborative environment where creativity and learning take centre stage, and the work is as fun as it is rewarding.

Find out more about us at https://elula.ai/team/.




The role

At Elula, data is the foundation of our AI-as-a-Service platform. As a Senior Data Engineer, you'll be the person who truly understands that data: where it comes from, what it means, how it behaves, and how to shape it into something reliable and useful.

Most of your best work will happen before you write any code; in understanding what the data represents, where it came from, and how it fits into our products. You'll spend your time interrogating complex datasets, designing thoughtful data models, and building the transformation logic that turns raw inputs into trusted, high-quality outputs powering our models and products. You won't just move data - you'll make sense of it.

You'll work autonomously on open-ended problems alongside data scientists, software engineers, and customer-facing teams, contributing across the full data lifecycle from ingestion and modelling through to validation and delivery. We're a collaborative, ambitious team, and we're looking for someone who takes genuine ownership, communicates clearly across disciplines, and isn't afraid to ask the hard questions about the data in front of them.

To be successful in this role, you will be expected to demonstrate the following skills and experience.
 

What You’ll Bring

Data transformation and modelling

A deep curiosity for data - you naturally interrogate datasets to understand their shape, quality, and fitness for purpose before building anything.

Strong applied data modelling skills, designing structures that serve analytical, operational and downstream use cases well.

The ability to develop and optimise complex SQL across large datasets, with a sharp eye for correctness, performance and long-term maintainability.

Comfortable using Python to automate workflows and building reusable tooling that makes the team more effective.

Ownership and judgement

You own your work end-to-end, from design decisions through to ongoing reliability, exercising sound technical judgement when trade-offs arise and proactively keeping stakeholders informed.

You manage your own priorities and delivery with discipline, and help your team do the same without waiting to be asked.

When something's wrong with the data, you dig in, diagnose it systematically, and fix it pragmatically, then share what you learned so the same issue doesn't come back.

 Technical leadership

You communicate complex data concepts clearly to both technical colleagues and non-technical stakeholders - translating between the two comes naturally.

You actively support and uplift junior engineers, sharing context, approaches and best practices generously.

You bring curiosity and a continuous improvement mindset, staying across new tools and techniques and contributing to raising the bar for the whole team.

You know how to get the most out of AI-assisted development tools like Claude Code - using them as an amplifier to think bigger and spend your energy on problems that genuinely require human judgment.




Nice to Have

While the following are not required, they are highly valued.

A background in a quantitative discipline such as computer science, mathematics or statistics.

Experience working in the financial services industry (e.g. banking, insurance or superannuation)


Benefits

Elula’s people are at the core of all we do. Our benefits are just one way we show that.

Enjoy flexible working hours, and work from home two days per week, to better balance your personal and professional life.

Experience the freedom of working anywhere in Australia up to five days annually.

Give back to what matters to you with extra paid annual leave for volunteering.

Put your health first with an allowance for fitness and wellbeing.

Drive your development with financial support for approved training courses.

Level up with matched funding for career coaching.




Interested?

If you're excited about shaping the future of AI, this is where you belong! Submit your CV and cover letter telling us why you're the right fit for Elula and what makes you special!

We're committed to fostering a culture of inclusivity and equality. All are welcome to apply for our roles because we truly believe that diversity is a game changer.

AEMO at the Heart of Energy   

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role 

The WA Market Reforms team sits within the Western Australia division and is responsible for the working with the Western Australian Government to define and implement policy change to the Western Australian Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM) and WA Gas Bulletin Board. 

You will work autonomously to lead the analysis, design and implementation of complex process and system changes to execute the WA Governments energy policy across AEMOs systems and processes. As a subject matter authority on power system engineering practices, you will provide expert advice to the WA Government to support the development of rule changes and lead the subsequent implementation of the policy within AEMOs systems and processes. 

What you will be doing  

Working collaboratively with internal stakeholders and WA Government rule makers to provide expert technical advice, business understanding, and regulatory requirements to support the development of solutions that align to the policy intent.

Leverage subject matter expertise of markets, systems and processes to proactively identify and manage complex technical, operational, regulatory and policy risks across power system engineering frameworks, supported by robust quantitative and qualitative analysis.

Lead the identification and analysis of changes to procedures, processes and/or systems resulting from regulatory change.

Provide direction to projects during feasibility and support project resources during implementation by providing expert understanding of the ESM and SWIS Technical rules, mechanics of electricity generating systems, and a technical understanding of AEMOs systems and processes.

Coach and supporting team members to develop expert technical advice and business acumen on complex cross functional technical, operational, regulatory or policy issues affecting AEMOs functions. 

What you will bring to the role  

Experienced energy sector professional with a comprehensive understanding of the political and regulatory framework in which the WA energy industry operates and AEMOs operations within these energy markets and power systems.

Significant experience in power systems in the Energy sector, ideally but not limited to experience interpreting and operating in compliance with technical documents and/or legal instruments, such as rules and regulations.

Demonstrated technical experience in implementation of complex technical, regulatory or operational process in IT systems.

Proven qualitative and quantitative analytical skills and ability to lead the development of high-quality written reports and deliver effective presentations which communicate highly technical, complicated concepts or issues to audiences with a range of backgrounds.

Experience in leadership roles in small teams is viewed favourably. 

In return some of our benefits to you 

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options 

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more. 

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.  

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility). 

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP).  

About Our Process  

AEMO values diversity and inclusivity in the workplace, welcoming applications from all backgrounds without regard to age, disability, gender, sexual orientation, parental status, race, religion and our First Nation peoples. We are dedicated to accommodating applicants' needs during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing tal•••@aemo.com.au.

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role

The WA Market Reforms team sits within the Western Australia division and is responsible for the working with the Western Australian Government to define and implement policy change to the Western Australian Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM) and WA Gas Bulletin Board. 

You will work autonomously to lead the analysis, design and implementation of complex process and system changes to execute the WA Governments energy policy across AEMOs systems and processes. As a subject matter authority on energy settlement practices, you will provide expert advice to the WA Government to support the development of rule changes and lead the subsequent implementation of the policy within AEMOs systems and processes. 

What you will be doing

Leverage subject matter expertise of markets, systems and processes to proactively identify and manage complex technical, operational, regulatory and policy risks across metering, settlement, prudentials, and retail market functions, supported by robust quantitative and qualitative analysis.

Lead the identification and analysis of changes to procedures, processes and/or systems resulting from regulatory change, including elaborating settlement policy as mathematical formula.

Provide direction to projects during feasibility and support project resources during implementation by providing expert understanding of the ESM and GSI rules, mechanics of electricity and gas systems, and a technical understanding of AEMOs systems and processes.

Coach and supporting team members to develop expert technical advice and business acumen on complex cross functional technical, operational, regulatory or policy issues affecting AEMOs functions.

What you will bring to the role  

Experienced energy sector professional with a comprehensive understanding of the WA energy markets and power systems and demonstrated experience in project delivery of IT systems and process.

Significant experience in a commercial or regulatory environment, ideally but not limited to experience in the Energy sector, and experience interpreting and operating in compliance with technical documents and/or legal instruments, such as rules and regulations.

Proven qualitative and quantitative analytical skills, with the ability to deal with a range of complex issues concurrently, propose technically resourceful, evidenced based solutions to issues and execute solutions in a constructive and timely manner.

Proficient expertise with developing mathematical equations and notations using markup languages, like LaTeX.

Experience related to metering, prudentials and settlements in the WEM is highly desirable.

Job Title: Lecturer / Senior Lecturer in Quantitative Finance
Ad Text:

Job Title: Senior Consultant, Asset Management and Performance

You'll lead packages of work within larger multi-disciplinary projects, taking ownership of your workstream from analysis through to recommendations - and building the client relationships that go with it.




Your work will include:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Experience producing high-quality intelligence reports and briefings.

Artificial Intelligence Specialist

Do you want to protect future generations?

Protect tomorrow together at the NSW EPA

Join a purpose-led organisation and make a difference to the environment and our community
Work on issues that matter in a supportive, collaborative and creative environment
Environment Officer Class 11
Remuneration starting from $144,018 plus superannuation

One ongoing role available
Role based in Parramatta
The opportunity

The Artificial Intelligence Specialist leads the design, development and implementation of AI-driven solutions that enhance the EPA’s regulatory, operational and environmental intelligence capabilities. The role supports the agency’s digital transformation by applying advanced analytics, machine learning and automation to improve environmental monitoring, compliance, decision-making and service delivery.

About you

To be successful in this role, you will have the following key knowledge and experience:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to assess data quality, identify patterns, and propose evidence-based solutions.
Demonstrated experience designing, developing, training, evaluating and deploying machine learning and artificial intelligence models to solve complex business or operational problems.
Proficiency in programming languages and tools relevant to machine learning and data analysis (e.g. Python, R, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, SQL).
Experience working with large and diverse datasets, including data processing, feature engineering, model optimisation and validation.
Experience applying cloud-based and/or on-premise machine learning platforms and deployment tools (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP, containerisation technologies).
Ability to communicate complex technical concepts, model outputs and data insights clearly and effectively to technical and non-technical stakeholders

Also, it is an essential requirement of this role for the successful candidate to hold a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, Mathematics, or related discipline, or equivalent professional experience.

About us

As the environmental steward and regulator of our State we are committed to a sustainable future.

Our people are proud to play a role in protecting the environment of NSW. We partner with Aboriginal people, industry and the wider community to protect, restore and enhance our diverse ecosystems.

We bring scientific expertise, experience and actions to inspire innovative solutions as we transition our state to a circular economy.

As we respond to urgent current issues, including climate change, we educate and support our stakeholders to create an environmentally healthy future.

Join us on our mission to protect tomorrow together.

Why work for us?

EPA people are energised by our purpose and focussed on the environment and we invite all our team members to:

Be part of protecting what matters – be purpose led making a meaningful impact to the environment, economy and communities delivering for a better NSW
Do more and be inspired together – be members of passionate united teams who protect the environment and each other through care, trust and collaboration.
Bring who you are and grow with us - we put people first creating a culture where everyone can be themselves, respecting and developing our diverse skills & aspirations and prioritising our safety and wellbeing.

Some additional benefits to working for us include:

A competitive salary and leave entitlements, salary packaging to save tax, fitness passport and free & confidential counselling services
Opportunities and pathways for learning and developing your skills with access to online and face to face training and mentoring
Flexible work arrangements
Opportunities to make an impact and help shape the future for our environment
Interesting and challenging work aligning with personal values that allow you to reach your full potential
Recognition programs

In our inclusive and accessible workplace, differences are embraced, contributions are valued, and everyone has a sense of connection and belonging. The EPA supports various employee affinity groups, practice flexible working, offer job share and workplace adjustments.

The EPA is an independent statutory authority which sits within the NSW Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW)

For more information on the work we do at NSW EPA, go to www.epa.nsw.gov.au/your-environment

To apply

If excited by the information above, we look forward to receiving your application, including a copy of your resume (max. 5 pages) and cover letter (max. 1 page) expressing your interest and suitability for the role.

As part of your application, you will be required to provide a response (max. 300 words) to the following question:

Describe a time where you designed, deployed or advised on an AI solution. What technologies models or tools did you use and why did you select these? What steps did you take to ensure the solution was ethical transparent explainable and compliant?

The Opportunity

(Private Equity, Private Credit, Venture Capital, Infrastructure and Property).
You will also act as a trusted internal adviser, helping lift capability across the business while building strong relationships with fund managers, advisers and clients.
  
Key Responsibilities

Attend relevant industry events and forums
Platform Development & Governance

We are seeking a commercially minded investment professional with genuine alternatives capability.

Strong relationships across fund managers and market participants
Excellent communication and presentation skills

Why This Role?

Join a respected wealth manager with a premium high-net-worth client base
Work directly with senior investment leadership
Build and shape a growing alternatives capability
Strong culture of collaboration, partnership and excellence
Genuine long-term career opportunity in a growing segment of the market
